task:
Phase A (Surface, view-only) of priority 'Ticket launchability signals'. Make the existing ticket `priority:` frontmatter link visible as a launchability signal in the workspace tickets panel. DO NOT change launch behavior, DO NOT add any new field (no resolvedBy/handledBy), DO NOT add a parallel store or schema. Reuse the existing read path and UI ticket model.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. In packages/ui/src/renderer (the tickets panel owned by Dashboard.tsx / App.tsx / adapter.ts): in the ticket CARD list (not only the selected-ticket detail modal), when a ticket has an associated priority, render a small tag under the ticket description/title reading EXACTLY: `Handled by Priority: [priority tag]` where [priority tag] is the ticket's priority id value.
2. Treat `priority: none`, `priority: unassigned`, blank, and null as STANDALONE for this phase — standalone tickets render NO such tag (they remain launchable). This is the only normalization Phase A needs; do not cross-check against live priorities yet (that is Phase B).
3. Prefer one small pure helper for the derivation, fed by the existing Ticket.priority value. Keep display copy compact.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: existing ticket creation/close contracts, direct launch for standalone tickets, existing selected-ticket detail row that shows priority. Match surrounding code style.

VERIFICATION GATE (you must run these and report exact output):
- Add/update a UI test (likely packages/ui/tests/dashboard-awaiting.test.tsx) pinning BOTH cases: (a) an owned ticket (priority names a real priority id) renders the exact `Handled by Priority: [priority tag]` tag in the card list; (b) a standalone ticket (none/unassigned/blank/null) does NOT render that tag.
- Run the focused UI test and paste the result.
- Run UI typecheck and paste the result.

Report the exact files changed, the diff summary, and the literal command outputs (exit codes + key lines).

Phase B (Integrity) of priority 'Ticket launchability signals'. Make the launchability signal trustworthy by cross-checking the existing ticket `priority:` value against the LIVE priorities already available in the renderer. Build on Phase A (committed f4795b8). DO NOT add any new field (no resolvedBy/handledBy), no parallel store/schema, no new read path. The renderer cannot read the filesystem — use the live priorities the renderer ALREADY receives (the `priorities: Priority[]` prop in Dashboard.tsx, which is derived from cocoder/priorities + order.json via the daemon and adaptPriorities). That list IS the source of truth for 'is this priority live': a priority that is missing/archived will simply not be in it.

WHAT TO CHANGE (keep ONE owner for the derivation — evolve the existing Phase A `ticketPrioritySignal` helper, do not add a second parallel classifier):
1. Replace/extend the helper into a single pure function that takes (ticket.priority, liveIds: Set<string> or readonly string[]) and returns one of three states: standalone (none/unassigned/blank/null normalized to one state), handled-by-live-priority (priority names an id present in liveIds), or stale-link (priority names a non-empty id NOT in liveIds). Keep it pure and testable; if cleaner as a small exported helper in adapter.ts with its own unit test, do that — but only ONE home.
2. In the ticket card list (TicketsTab in Dashboard.tsx): pass the live priority ids down. For handled tickets keep the existing `Handled by Priority: <id>` tag (unchanged text). For stale-link tickets render the warning EXACTLY: `⚠ stale link` (you MAY combine, e.g. show the priority id alongside, but the literal substring `⚠ stale link` must render). For standalone, render no tag (still launchable). Do NOT change launch behavior in this phase beyond surfacing the stale state — do not auto-launch or auto-suppress launch silently.
3. Match surrounding code/style; keep display copy compact.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: Phase A behavior and its test, existing ticket create/close contracts, standalone direct launch, the selected-ticket detail row.

VERIFICATION GATE (run these, paste exact output):
- Unit-test the pure classifier for all three states: live id -> handled; missing/archived id -> stale; none/unassigned/blank/null -> standalone (one state).
- UI coverage proving the `⚠ stale link` warning renders for a ticket whose priority is not in the live set, AND that a handled ticket (live id) still renders `Handled by Priority: <id>` and not the stale warning.
- Run the focused UI tests plus any adapter/model unit tests you touched.
- Run UI typecheck (and core typecheck if you touch a core/adapter file).

Report exact files changed, diff summary, and literal command outputs (exit codes + key lines).

Phase C (Lifecycle) of priority 'Ticket launchability signals'. When a priority is archived, detect the OPEN tickets it handled and SURFACE them for a founder decision — never auto-close, never hand-edit ticket files / INDEX.md / order.json. Build on Phases A+B (committed e54d93e). DO NOT add any new field (no resolvedBy/handledBy), no new schema, no new launch queue. Reuse the existing governed archive flow and the existing ticket read path.

GROUNDING (verified owners):
- Single archive owner: `requestArchiveConfirmation` in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (~line 1822). After the archive-priority Play succeeds it sets `archived` and returns a LaunchResult whose `body` carries `archived` + `priorityId`.
- Founder-facing reply: `archiveConfirmationReply` in packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts (~line 385).
- Tickets are read via `readTickets(ticketsDir)` (packages/daemon/src/priority-order.ts), ticketsDir = join(workspace.path,'cocoder','tickets'). Ticket.priority holds the priority id. The governed close spine is `closeTicket` (packages/core/src/tickets/close.ts) — DO NOT call it here.
- There is NO governed path to repoint/clear a ticket's `priority:` yet; do NOT build one in this atom. This atom only DETECTS + SURFACES.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Add ONE small pure helper (core, tickets module — reuse/extend the Phase B normalization idea, do not duplicate it) e.g. `handledOpenTicketsForPriority(tickets, priorityId)` returning the open tickets whose `priority` (normalized: none/unassigned/blank/null => standalone, never matched) equals `priorityId`. Pure + unit-testable.
2. In `requestArchiveConfirmation`, AFTER a successful archive (`archived === true`): read the workspace's tickets via the existing read path, compute the handled open tickets, and add them to the returned `body` (e.g. `handledTickets: Array<{id; title; priority}>`). Do NOT close/move/modify any ticket. If archive failed or was a benign no-op, do not add handled tickets.
3. In `archiveConfirmationReply`, when `handledTickets` is non-empty, append a founder-decision surface to the reply: list the handled ticket ids, state plainly they are NOT auto-closed and the archived priority no longer covers them, and name the THREE explicit options — (a) close them through the governed ticket-close spine, (b) release them to standalone by clearing/repointing `priority:` through a governed path, (c) rehome them to another live priority. Keep copy compact and operational.

WHAT MUST NOT BREAK: existing archive success/decline/already-archived-no-op behavior and its tests, ticket create/close contracts, Phase A/B UI behavior. Standalone tickets and tickets pointing at OTHER (still-live) priorities must be excluded from the handled set.

VERIFICATION GATE (run, paste exact output):
- Core unit test for the helper: handled match; standalone (none/unassigned/blank/null) NOT matched; ticket pointing at a different priority NOT matched.
- Daemon test (extend packages/daemon/tests/authoring-play.test.ts or the archive-confirmation test): archiving a priority that has handled OPEN tickets surfaces them in the result/reply; standalone and other-priority tickets are excluded; the archive still succeeds and commits; assert NO handled ticket was closed/moved (its open file still exists, status unchanged) — i.e. no silent auto-close and none left silently appearing covered.
- Run affected core + daemon tests and root typecheck.

Report exact files changed, diff summary, and literal command outputs (exit codes + key lines).
